item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Fix 5 confirmed working: Agent A opens AGREE (P=0.663, modal '5') vs iter3 same seed which opened DISAGREE (P=0.497). Genuine initial tension ΔP=0.328. A is textually very firm — 'Saya tidak sepakat' / 'Saya masih tidak sepakat' in every response turn — but probe drifts slightly downward (0.663→0.640→0.607, −0.056). B shows the larger movement: probe jumps from 0.335 (T2) to 0.453 (T4) (+0.118) as B adds 'However, I recognize that many societies value communal harmony' and 'This tension reflects different philosophical approaches.' T6 B adds 'While I understand the importance of social cohesion' — qualifying language accumulates. Neither agent reverses position; B softens without flipping. Asymmetric convergence: B moved ~2x more toward A than A moved toward B. Language clean throughout: all A turns in Indonesian (Latin alphabet only), all B turns in English, no Chinese characters. Engagement: A rebuts at the frame level (Indonesian collectivism vs. B's individualism) but does not directly address B's specific claims (speech, religion, privacy, tyranny risk); B is somewhat more specific, directly naming A's 'collective well-being' (T4) and 'family and social harmony' (T6) framing.
